[2021-04-28 22:49] velizar_ (Name): it might be enough to have two voice chats and then each group has a driver, so the two drivers match against each other while everyone else gives advice, and the driver for each group ultimately makes the decision
[2021-04-28 22:50] velizar_ (Name): this could actually make you weaker than a single player because of coordination confusion, so you'd have to be organized
[2021-04-28 22:51] velizar_ (Name): also I'll be curious about what would happen if both teams are in the same chat, since Prismata has no hidden information
[2021-04-28 22:51] velizar_ (Name): or even if you have one team playing against itself
[2021-04-28 22:53] Deleted User (Deleted User): this might even allow a cooperative sync (as contrary to async) game.
[2021-04-28 22:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): that actually might be fun, but I think it would be more difficult and less motivating.
[2021-04-28 22:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): there are two cases I have against this:
1. having 2 channels helps to keep things more organized, so you can just continue your thoughts from last-round.
2. even though prismata has no hidden-info, I think that having an insight into the other team plans can give you a strategic advantage. I think it would be more interesting if both teams just think on their own and at the end of the game they can peek into the other's team thread and discuss what could've been done differently etc..
[2021-04-28 22:59] Deleted User (Deleted User): I think this idea is possibly more doable to execute, personally I always prefer text > voice, and this allows the game to keep going on asynchronously. while if you go the voice route you'd possibly have timezone issues if you still wish to play async, so it is more suitable for a sync route.
